title: DOTDAY page archetype manifest
description: A frozen, versioned recipe for one page style. Content files opt in by declaring archetype: '<id>@<version>'; validate-archetype.mjs then enforces this manifest against the page. Two enforcement modes, usable together: 'sequence' (an ordered list of slots the page's sections must match - for landing archetypes where the structure is the design) and 'rules' (order-light constraints on the block/section list - for editorial surfaces like blog where the body varies within rules).

version.description: Frozen once published. A redesign is a new version; existing pages keep validating against the version they declare.

surface.description: Which content collection this archetype governs. Landing archetypes constrain page.sections; blog archetypes constrain post.blocks.

description.description: What this page style is for and when to reach for it.

reference.description: Slug of the approved reference implementation this archetype was frozen from.

sequence.description: Ordered slot recipe. The page's section list is matched against these slots in order: each slot consumes min..max consecutive sections whose _type is in the slot's types. Unmatched sections and unfilled required slots are errors.

slot.description: Human name for the slot, e.g. 'opener', 'proof', 'closer'.

types.description: Section _type values allowed to fill this slot.

min.description: Minimum sections this slot must consume. 0 makes the slot optional.

max.description: Maximum sections this slot may consume.

rules.description: Order-light constraints on the section/block list.

allowedTypes.description: If present, every _type on the page must be in this list.
